mnemonics: Exit Cleanly, Or Processes Leak (E.C.O.P.L.) - Reminds you that exit() ensures clean process termination, preventing resource leaks.
keyPoints: The exit() function terminates the calling process and returns a status value to the operating system.
All open file descriptors are closed, and any child processes are inherited by the init process (reparenting).
The exit status (an integer) is available to the parent process via wait() or waitpid().
commonMistakes: Confusing exit() with _exit() or _Exit(); exit() performs cleanup like flushing stdio buffers, while _exit() does not.
Forgetting that the exit status is only the lower 8 bits of the int argument, so values above 255 are truncated.
